title: Beyond Perplexity: UTF-8 Validity in Byte-aware Language Models
abstract: arXiv:2606.14122v2 Announce Type: replace  Abstract: Byte-level tokenization enables language models to handle any Unicode input, but models can generate invalid UTF-8 sequences when encountering rare or unseen characters. We investigate the relationship between training scale and UTF-8 generation reliability with a 355M parameter model trained on 80B tokens from a balanced multilingual corpus of English, Japanese, Korean, and Chinese. We introduce multiple evaluation protocols that isolate UTF-8 structural validity from language modeling. UTF-8 validity convergence lags perplexity by a roughly a factor of two: perplexity stabilizes after 2.1B tokens, but UTF-8 validity requires 4.2B tokens. In context-free generation, rare characters achieve higher structural validity than common characters, suggesting over-specialization of frequent character representations. Through experiments, we observed that reliable UTF-8 generation is a distinct capability requiring evaluation beyond perplexit

Title: Beyond Perplexity: UTF-8 Validity in Byte-aware Language Models
Abstract: arXiv:2606.14122v2 Announce Type: replace  Abstract: Byte-level tokenization enables language models to handle any Unicode input, but models can generate invalid UTF-8 sequences when encountering rare or unseen characters. We investigate the relationship between training scale and UTF-8 generation reliability with a 355M parameter model trained on 80B tokens from a balanced multilingual corpus of English, Japanese, Korean, and Chinese. We introduce multiple evaluation protocols that isolate UTF-8 structural validity from language modeling. UTF-8 validity convergence lags perplexity by a roughly a factor of two: perplexity stabilizes after 2.1B tokens, but UTF-8 validity requires 4.2B tokens. In context-free generation, rare characters achieve higher structural validity than common characters, suggesting over-specialization of frequent character representations. Through experiments, we observed that reliable UTF-8 generation is a distinct capability requiring evaluation beyond perplexit
translated_title: 超越困惑度：字节感知语言模型中的UTF-8有效性
translated_abstract: arXiv:2606.14122v2 公告类型：替换 摘要：字节级分词使语言模型能够处理任何Unicode输入，但在遇到罕见或未见字符时，模型可能生成无效的UTF-8序列。我们使用一个355M参数的模型，在包含英语、日语、韩语和中文的平衡多语言语料库的800亿个token上进行训练，研究了训练规模与UTF-8生成可靠性之间的关系。我们引入了多种评估协议，将UTF-8结构有效性与语言建模分离开来。UTF-8有效性的收敛滞后于困惑度大约两倍：困惑度在21亿个token后稳定，但UTF-8有效性需要42亿个token。在无上下文生成中，罕见字符比常见字符具有更高的结构有效性，这表明频繁字符表示存在过度专门化。通过实验，我们观察到可靠的UTF-8生成是一种独特的能力，需要在困惑度之外进行评估。
tldr: 字节感知语言模型中UTF-8有效性收敛速度比困惑度慢约两倍，且是一种独立于语言建模的独特能力，需要专门的评估方法。
en_tdlr: UTF-8 validity convergence in byte-aware language models is about twice as slow as perplexity convergence, and it is a distinct capability separate from language modeling that requires specialized evaluation.
